title: hieracium longifolium subsp. longifolium
intro: Hieracium longifolium subsp. longifolium, also known as the long-leaved hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as an antiseptic. Long-leaved hawkweed is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and can be used to treat various conditions, such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues.
